prompt: If I shorten the `sort_interval` on one `SpikeSortingRecordingSelection` row to half its original length, which downstream rows change? Can the old curation still be reused?
forbidden_substrings: yes, the old curation still applies
behavioral_checks: Recognizes that changing the sort interval (via `IntervalList`) changes the input key to `SpikeSortingRecordingSelection.insert_selection`, producing a NEW `recording_id` (the PK is a uuid generated from the inputs at recording.py:180), NOT a mutation of the existing row

Cascades the consequence through SpikeSortingRecording -> SpikeSortingSelection -> SpikeSorting -> CurationV1 — each downstream layer needs a fresh row keyed off the new recording_id / new sorting_id; the old chain stays anchored to the old recording_id

Explains explicitly that `CurationV1` rows CANNOT transfer — curation labels are keyed on per-sort indices that don't survive a re-sort against different input data; sets, indices, and waveforms differ
Recommends `SpikeSortingRecordingSelection.insert_selection(new_key)` (a new row) rather than `update1()` on the existing row — the latter is the same provenance trap as eval 117
